game.invalidMove: This is not a valid move

noMoves.title: No legal moves
noMoves.pawn: The square straight ahead is blocked and there are no enemy pieces to capture diagonally. (Pawns never capture straight; from the starting rank a two-step leap needs both squares ahead empty.)
noMoves.knight: Every L-shaped landing square is off the board or occupied by a friendly piece.
noMoves.king: All neighboring squares are blocked by a friendly piece or the edge of the board.
noMoves.slider: Every direction along a rank, file, or diagonal is blocked, with no empty square to enter or enemy to capture.

tutorial.pawnRules: Pawns only move straight forward: one square at a time, or two from the starting rank when both squares ahead are empty. They capture only on the forward diagonals—never straight ahead.

pawnSituation.start_double_available: Still on the starting rank with a clear two-square path—you may move one or two squares forward.
pawnSituation.start_single_only: Still on the starting rank, but the second square ahead is occupied—you can advance only one square forward (captures on diagonals still apply).
pawnSituation.start_forward_blocked: The square straight ahead is blocked—you cannot step forward until it clears; diagonal captures still work if an enemy is there.
pawnSituation.advanced: Already past the starting rank—you may move only one square forward (or capture diagonally).

tutorial.rookRules: Rooks slide along ranks and files until blocked. They capture by landing on the first enemy in a direction.

rookSituation.corner: In a corner, this rook can extend along at most two directions along the board.
rookSituation.edge: On the edge, at most three directions stay on the board until blocked.
rookSituation.inner: Away from the edge, all four directions may be used along straight lines until a piece stops the ray.

tutorial.knightRules: Knights move in an L-shape and may jump over pieces. The landing square must be empty or hold an enemy to capture—not a friendly piece.

knightSituation.corner: Near a corner, several L-shape landings fall off the board, so fewer jumps are normally legal.
knightSituation.edge: On the edge, some knight moves leave the board and cannot be used.
knightSituation.inner: In the center, more full knight moves are usually possible; friendly pieces on landing squares still block.

tutorial.bishopRules: Bishops slide diagonally until blocked. They always stay on the same square color they started from.
tutorial.queenRules: The queen combines rook and bishop: any distance along a rank, file, or diagonal until blocked, capturing the first enemy in that line.

queenSituation.mixed: From here it can still use both straight and diagonal lines in at least one legal direction.
queenSituation.orthogonal_only: Right now all its legal destinations lie on ranks or files only—diagonal rays are blocked before any legal landing.
queenSituation.diagonal_only: Right now every legal destination is on a diagonal—straight ranks/files are blocked before any legal landing.

tutorial.kingRules: The king steps one square in any direction to an empty square or to capture an enemy. (This app does not yet enforce check or castling.)

kingSituation.corner: In a corner, fewer neighboring squares exist on the board.
kingSituation.edge: On the edge, fewer adjacent squares are on the board than in the center.
kingSituation.inner: In the center, up to eight neighbors can be considered if empty or capturable.
